name: Mavodelpar STRIDE did not show a 12-minute walk advantage over placebo
summary: The main posted PPAR-delta primary mitochondrial myopathy trial is a boundary condition for druglike mitochondrial-biogenesis translation.
statement: In the posted STRIDE phase 2 primary mitochondrial myopathy results, mavodelpar did not outperform placebo on change in 12-minute walk distance over 24 weeks.
quantitative_note: ClinicalTrials.gov reports mean 12-minute walk change of 26.75 m with mavodelpar versus 30.89 m with placebo; fatigue-score change was -0.98 with mavodelpar versus -2.60 with placebo.
caveats: The source is a trial-registry results record, not a peer-reviewed paper in this staged pass.
Primary mitochondrial myopathy is a rare-disease context and not a healthy-aging trial.
The result limits broad PPAR-delta mitochondrial-function claims but does not negate exercise-based human evidence.
